title: vandenboschia ramitricha
intro: Vandenboschia ramitricha, also known as the forked spleenwort or forked polypody, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This epiphytic plant is native to Southeast Asia and Australia, and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Asian medicine. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system, offering health benefits such as improved wound healing and reduced inflammation. In herbal preparations, Vandenboschia ramitricha is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin problems.
